summary: Current source and registry state do not admit any OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, clustered-storage successor, superiority, or comparator-performance claim for distributed TideFS behavior.

current_comparable_envelope.cluster_topology: No topology is admitted for successor comparison. The current evidence set does not bind a node count, quorum shape, role layout, placement domain, or witness/fencing arrangement to a validated distributed storage behavior.
current_comparable_envelope.membership_assumptions: No membership assumption is admitted for successor comparison. The current evidence set does not prove live membership convergence, fencing, split-brain exclusion, or membership-change behavior for data ownership.
current_comparable_envelope.network_mode: No network mode is admitted for successor comparison. TCP, RDMA, loopback, single-host multi-process, and multi-host modes remain non-comparable until the run evidence names the mode and proves the data path under that mode.
current_comparable_envelope.media_assumptions: No media class is admitted for successor comparison. Local block devices, loop devices, files, NVMe, HDD, mixed media, and remote media remain non-comparable until the evidence names the media and proves ownership, failure, and recovery effects.
current_comparable_envelope.workload_classes: No distributed workload class is admitted for successor comparison. Metadata, data read/write, sync write, fsync, block export, snapshot, rebuild, scrub, degraded read, recovery, rebalance, and mixed workload claims remain non-comparable.
current_comparable_envelope.measurement_attribution: No distributed measurement attribution is admitted. Current source-model or prototype rows cannot be attributed to a product-equivalent TideFS distributed storage mode, incumbent system, media class, or transport mode.
current_comparable_envelope.fault_model: No fault model is admitted for successor comparison. Node loss, process loss, network partition, packet loss, RDMA failure, device loss, media error, stale membership, crash/restart, and partial recovery cases remain unproven.
current_comparable_envelope.recovery_and_degraded_visible_states: No recovery or degraded-visible state is admitted for successor comparison. The current evidence does not prove client-visible behavior, operator-visible refusal, rebuild, re-replication, scrub, or data availability under degraded operation.
non_comparable_cases: Any OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, clustered-storage, distributed-storage, successor, superiority, or comparator-performance wording.
Any benchmark row that lacks current multi-process distributed evidence tied to the exact topology, transport, media, workload, fault, recovery, and attribution envelope.
Any source-model, placement, transport, or cluster prototype evidence presented as product-equivalent distributed storage behavior.

minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.cluster_topology: A future admitting artifact must name node count, roles, quorum/fencing model, placement domains, and whether nodes are single-host processes or distinct hosts.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.membership_assumptions: A future admitting artifact must name membership source, convergence expectations, failure detector behavior, fencing boundary, and split-brain exclusion evidence.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.network_mode: A future admitting artifact must name TCP, RDMA, loopback, or mixed transport mode and bind measurements to that mode.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.media_assumptions: A future admitting artifact must name media type, device ownership, local or remote media placement, and any durability/cache assumptions.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.workload_classes: A future admitting artifact must name each workload class and avoid carrying one class as proof for another.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.measurement_attribution: A future admitting artifact must separate TideFS overhead, transport cost, media cost, recovery cost, client cost, and incumbent-system comparison setup.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.fault_model: A future admitting artifact must name injected and observed faults, expected visible outcomes, and refused cases.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.recovery_and_degraded_visible_states: A future admitting artifact must name client-visible and operator-visible states during degraded service and recovery.
minimum_future_evidence_before_compariso.non_comparable_cases: A future admitting artifact must list every out-of-envelope case that remains blocked.
